title: sidalcea oregana subsp. spicata
intro: Sidalcea oregana subsp. spicata, also known as the Oregon checkermallow, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family that is native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are thought to be due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Sidalcea oregana subsp. spicata include its ability to soothe sore throats and reduce fever, as well as its potential to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. Sidalcea oregana subsp. spicata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies for its medicinal properties.
